appendix.title: Frequently Asked Questions
appendix.para: This FAQ is provided with the hopes to be helpful for users using SilkPage. The information is updated as the questions and answers becomes appearant to the SilkPage team.

answer.para: SilkPage is for everyone who believes in Open Standards, XML and quality. The only pre-requisite to use SilkPage is some basic knowledge of XML, to be able to encode your website content. While the SilkPage software is well documented, it's (cuurently) based on a command line executions. If you can't sand command line executions, then SilkPage is not for you.

question.para: I don't know anything about XML, can I still use SilkPage

answer.para: No, to use SilkPage, you need to provide your Website content in XML format. Therefore, you need to know the XML (very) basics. Refer to to get started.

question.para: What are the differeces between SilkPage and DocBook Website

answer.para:
SilkPage is based on DocBook Website. Both applications share almost the identical features, such as Website publishing. However, SilkPage provides some extra features:
Generates (WaSP) compliant XHTML output
Provides a better Customization via and customization layers.
Prvides a better support for XML feeds such as and .
Automates the build and website publising by using tasks.

question.para: What is the relation between SilkPage releases and DocBook Website

answer.para: Piror to every release, SilkPage team takes the current version of releases, makes modifications (if necessary), and prepares its own release. The DocBook Website output may have a different look/feel than SilkPage output. Moreover, SilkPage provides extra features not present in DocBook Website.

question.para: How do I create my first Website

listitem.para: Create a brand new site from scratch
Structure your Website by organizing your XML content
Create XML source pages and content

question.para: How do I migerate my website from DocBook Website to SilkPage

answer.para: SilkPage is based on . The only thing you need to do to port your website to SilkPage is to copy the content of your website under /src/en. Note that en is the two letter ISO code for languages. You can use the language of your choice. Refer to for valid language codes.

question.para: How can I change the link labels of XML feed items generated by SilkPage on top of each webpage

answer.para: Refer to to learn how you can control this feature.

question.para: How can I change the link labels of the footer items generated by SilkPage

answer.para: Refer to to learn how you can control this feature.

question.para: SilkPage generates a Google based search field for my website, how can I turn it off

answer.para: Refer to to learn how you can control this feature.

RSS is a format for syndicating news and the content of news-like sites, including major news sites like Wired, news-oriented community sites like Slashdot, and personal weblogs. Refer to
All About RSS
for a complete desciption.

question.para: What are all RSS formats and versions ? Which one should I choose

answer.para:
There are 0.90 and 0.91 by
Netscape , 1.0 by
RSS-DEV
and 0.9x and 2.0 by
UserLand Software . Refer to the
What's RSS Article to find out more.

question.para: What is required to run SilkPage
How do I install the SilkPage software

answer.para: It's simple, for all platforms you can download the .zip archive file and unpack it on the hard disk using your favorite tool. See for more information.

question.para: On what platforms SilkPage can be installed and run

answer.para: Any operating system supporting Java (J2SE), Standard Edition. However, it should be mentioned that we use GNU/Linux as our reference operating system.

question.para: I can't even run SilkPage with the config target to test my installation, what's wrong

answer.para: The most common hiccup is when JAVA_HOME or/and ANT_HOME are not set. Make sure that you have done the following before installing and running SilkPage:

listitem.para: Download the J2SE release (version 1.4 or later) from:
Install the J2SE according to the instructions included with the release.
Set an environment variable JAVA_HOME to the pathname of the directory into which you installed the J2SE release.
Download the release (version 1.6.1 or later) from:
Install Ant according to the instructions included with the release.
Set an environment variable ANT_HOME to the pathname of the directory into which you installed the ANT release.

answer.para: In addition, make sure that are set correctly.
